title: Treasure Beneath Their Feet: The Clay Soldiers' Secret
story: On this very day in 1974, farmers digging a well in China got the surprise of their lives! Instead of finding water, they stumbled upon the amazing Terracotta Army – thousands of life-sized clay soldiers buried underground for over 2,000 years. Can you believe each soldier has a different face? That's right! These ancient warriors were created to protect China's first emperor, Qin Shi Huang, in the afterlife. Imagine having your own personal army made of clay! The soldiers stood silently underground for centuries, like players in the world's longest game of freeze tag, until those lucky farmers accidentally found them. Today, this clay army is one of the world's most amazing treasures that kids from all around the globe travel to see.

title: Brazil's First Capital: The Birthday of Salvador!
story: Guess what happened on March 29th way back in 1549? The very first capital city of Brazil was born! Salvador da Bahia was built on a high cliff overlooking a beautiful blue bay, kind of like creating the ultimate tree fort with the best view in the neighborhood. The Portuguese settlers who founded the city thought it was the perfect spot to run their new colony from. Salvador was like the command center for Brazil for more than two centuries before Rio de Janeiro took over the job. What makes this city super special is that it still has colorful buildings from hundreds of years ago, with music and dancing in the streets that blend African, European, and native Brazilian traditions. It's like having a birthday party that's been going on for almost 500 years!

title: The Light Bulb Wizard's Birthday
story: March 29th marks the birthday of a boy who would grow up to light up our world! Back in 1853, Elihu Thomson was born, and though you might not know his name like Edison's, this curious kid became an inventor who helped create many of the electrical gadgets we can't live without today. Thomson was like a real-life wizard who figured out how to tame electricity, inventing better electric lights, motors, and even X-ray machines! He founded what would later become part of General Electric, one of the biggest companies in the world. Imagine being so smart that you end up with over 700 patents—that's like coming up with a new brilliant idea every month for over 50 years! The next time you flip a light switch, remember Elihu Thomson, whose birthday candles would have needed quite a lot of electricity to light!

title: Soccer Star's Story: Jlloyd's Journey
story: On this day in 1981, a future soccer star named Jlloyd Samuel was born in Trinidad. Like many kids who dream of playing in the big leagues, Jlloyd moved to England when he was young and worked super hard to become a professional footballer. His determination paid off! He got to play for famous teams like Aston Villa and Bolton Wanderers in the English Premier League – imagine running onto the field with thousands of fans cheering for you! Jlloyd was known for being lightning-fast and for his skill at stopping the other team from scoring. He even represented Trinidad and Tobago, proudly wearing his home country's colors. Sadly, Jlloyd's story ended too soon when he passed away in 2018, but his amazing journey from a Caribbean island to becoming a Premier League defender continues to inspire young players who have big soccer dreams of their own.

title: The Oddball Boxer Who Pretended to Be Crazy
story: On March 29, 1928, a boy named Vincent Gigante was born who would grow up to become one of the strangest characters in American history. Starting out as a boxer with the funny nickname

Vincent later joined the mob and eventually became a powerful crime boss. But here's the really weird part – for decades, he wandered around New York City in his bathrobe and slippers, mumbling to himself and acting confused! It was all a clever trick to fool the police into thinking he was too crazy to be running a criminal organization. Imagine fooling everyone at school by pretending to not understand the rules of tag while secretly being the best player! His act was so convincing that many people believed him, but the FBI wasn't fooled forever. Vincent's strange disguise earned him another nickname:
movies you might hear your parents talk about.

title: Rock 'n' Roll Traveler: Billy's Booming Beat
story: Did you know that on this day in 1946, a baby boy was born in England who would grow up to rock Australia? That's right! Billy Thorpe started out as a regular English kid but moved to Australia when he was just nine years old. There, he discovered he had an amazing talent for making music that made people want to dance and cheer! Billy became one of Australia's biggest rock stars, playing his electric guitar so loudly that his band was called

– imagine being so noisy that you become famous for it! Like a musical explorer, Billy kept changing his sound throughout his life, trying everything from pop songs to hard rock to electronic music. He was like a chef who keeps inventing new recipes that everyone loves. Though he passed away in 2007, Australians still turn up the volume whenever a Billy Thorpe song comes on the radio!

title: K-Pop Princess: Irene's Birthday Spotlight
story: Hey, did you know that March 29th is a super special day for K-pop fans around the world? That's because on this day in 1991, Irene was born in South Korea! Before becoming the famous leader of the girl group Red Velvet, she was just a regular girl named Bae Joo-hyun who loved to dance and sing. Imagine practicing dance moves for hours and hours until you could perform them perfectly – that's what Irene did to become the star she is today! Not only can she bust amazing dance moves, but she also appears on TV shows and in movies. Irene is known for being super organized and taking care of the other members in her group, kind of like the team captain who makes sure everyone has their water bottles and knows the game plan. With millions of fans who call themselves 'ReVeluvs,' Irene's birthday is celebrated with special events and projects by kids and teens all around the globe!

title: Piano Fingers: The Birthday of a Keyboard Wizard
story: Way back on March 29, 1747, a baby boy named Johann Wilhelm Hässler was born who had the most amazing fingers in all of Germany! As he grew up, Johann discovered he could make piano keys dance like nobody else. Imagine having fingers so fast they could race across a keyboard like tiny athletes sprinting in the Olympics! Hässler became so good that he once had a musical

with the famous Mozart – that's like having a basketball contest against LeBron James! He traveled all over Europe showing off his incredible piano skills and wrote beautiful music that pianists still play today. Johann was also super clever with organs (the giant pianos in churches) and could fix them when they broke. Though he lived long before recording devices were invented, his music lives on in books and is still played by piano students who are trying to get their fingers to move as quickly as Hässler's did over 250 years ago!

title: From Tackles to Teaching: Joe's Powerful Play
story: On this day in 1949, a baby boy named Joe Ehrmann was born who would grow up to be a giant on the football field – and an even bigger hero off it! Joe became a super tough defensive lineman for the Baltimore Colts, using his strength to tackle opponents for 13 seasons in the NFL. But here's the cool twist: after his football days, Joe decided to use his powerful voice to help kids! He became a pastor and started teaching boys that being a real man isn't about being the biggest or toughest, but about showing kindness and standing up for others. Joe wrote books and travels around talking to kids and coaches about how sports should build character, not just muscles. He's like a football player who turned into a real-life superhero, fighting against bullying and teaching kids how to be good teammates in the game of life. Many people think Joe's work off the field is even more important than all the tackles he made as a pro!

title: The Honky-Tonk Piano King's Birthday Bash
story: Back on this day in 1909, a baby named Moon Mullican was born who would one day make pianos rock and roll before rock and roll was even a thing! With a cool nickname like

how could he not become famous? Growing up in Texas, Moon learned to play piano in a wild and energetic style that made it impossible for people to sit still. His fingers would bounce and dance across the keyboard like they were having their own party! Moon mixed country music with boogie-woogie and blues to create a sound that made people jump out of their seats and dance. He was like the DJ of his time, getting everyone on the dance floor! Many music experts say Moon helped invent rock and roll music years before it became popular. So next time you're dancing to your favorite songs, remember Moon Mullican, who was getting people grooving almost 100 years ago with just a piano and his amazing talent!
